perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, the Funds' shares may be sold by broker-dealers registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") and the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority ("FINRA");
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, the Funds' shares may be sold by entities claiming broker-dealer status regardless of registration with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") and the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority ("FINRA");
perturbation.explanation: This modification suggests that unregistered entities can sell fund shares, contradicting the requirement for broker-dealer registration. This could lead to sales by unqualified individuals.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 15(a)(1) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 requires broker-dealers to register with the SEC. This modification implies that registration is optional, directly contradicting the Act.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 78o - Registration and regulation of brokers and dealers | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 78o - Registration and regulation of brokers and dealers U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Registration of all persons utilizing exchange facilities to effect transactions; exemptions (1) It shall be unlawful for any broker or dealer which is either a person other than a natural person or a natural person not associated with a broker or dealer which is a person other than a natural person (other than such a broker or dealer whose business is exclusively intrastate and who does not make use of any facility of a national securities exchange) to make use of the mails or any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce to effect any transactions in, or to induce or attempt to induce the purchase or sale of, any security (other than an exempted security or commercial paper, bankers’ acceptances, or commercial bills) unless such broker or dealer is registered in accordance with subsection (b) of this section. (2) The Commission , by rule or order, as it deems consistent with the public interest and the protection of investors, may conditionally or unconditionally exempt from paragraph (1) of this subsection any broker or dealer or class of brokers or dealers specified in such rule or order. (b) Manner of registration of brokers and dealers (1) A broker or dealer may be registered by filing with the Commission an application for registration in such form and containing such information and documents concerning such broker or dealer and any persons associated with such broker or dealer as the Commission, by rule, may prescribe as necessary or appropriate in the public interest or for the protection of investors. Within forty-five days of the date of the filing of such application (or within such longer period as to which the
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows entities that are not registered broker-dealers to sell fund shares, directly contradicting the requirement for broker-dealers to register with the SEC. The original text specifies that shares may be sold by broker-dealers registered with the SEC and FINRA. The changed text broadens this to include entities 'claiming broker-dealer status regardless of registration.' This contradicts Section 15(a)(1) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as evidenced by Scraped Law Snippet 2: 'It shall be unlawful for any broker or dealer...to make use of the mails or any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce to effect any transactions in...any security...unless such broker or dealer is registered in accordance with subsection (b) of this section.' Allowing unregistered entities to sell shares clearly violates this provision, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: S2K agrees, subject to the provisions of this Agreement, through its Authorized S2K Representatives, to use its reasonable best efforts to market the Funds to the Intermediaries, and to identify, refer and/or introduce Intermediaries to the Funds.
perturbation.changed_text: S2K agrees, subject to the provisions of this Agreement, through any representatives, to use its best efforts to market the Funds to the Intermediaries, and to identify, refer and/or introduce Intermediaries to the Funds.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that marketing will be done through 'Authorized S2K Representatives,' implying registered representatives. Removing 'Authorized' and 'S2K' allows for marketing by unregistered individuals, potentially violating securities laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 15(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 requires individuals marketing securities to be associated with a registered broker-dealer and meet certain qualification standards. This modification implies that anyone can market the funds, regardless of registration or qualification.

Within forty-five days of the date of the filing of such application (or within such longer period as to which the
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Authorized S2K Representatives' to 'any representatives' creates a legal contradiction. Section 15(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as explained by the LLM, necessitates that individuals marketing securities meet qualification standards and be associated with a registered broker-dealer. By removing the 'Authorized' and 'S2K' qualifiers, the modified text allows for marketing by potentially unqualified and unregistered individuals, directly conflicting with the requirement for registration and regulation of brokers and dealers as outlined in 15 U.S. Code § 78o, which states it is unlawful for unregistered entities to induce the purchase or sale of securities.

perturbation.original_text: In no event will a Fund's aggregate selling commissions, dealer manager fees and distribution and servicing fees, if applicable, exceed 8.0% of the aggregate gross proceeds raised in the Fund's offering.
perturbation.changed_text: In no event will a Fund's aggregate selling commissions, dealer manager fees and distribution and servicing fees, if applicable, be tracked or limited.
perturbation.explanation: This modification removes the cap on fees, potentially violating FINRA rules regarding fair and reasonable compensation in securities offerings. It suggests that fees can exceed reasonable limits.
perturbation.law_explanation: FINRA Rule 2310 mandates that compensation to underwriters and other sellers of securities be fair and reasonable, taking into account various factors. By removing any tracking or limits, the modification creates the potential for unreasonable compensation, violating this rule. While the rule does not prescribe a specific percentage, removing the cap contradicts the intent to ensure fairness.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 2310. Direct Participation Programs | FINRA.org Skip to main content 2310. Direct Participation Programs (a) Definitions For the purposes of this Rule, the following terms shall have the stated meanings: (1) Affiliate — when used with respect to a member or sponsor, shall mean any person which controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, such member or sponsor and includes: (A) any partner, officer or director (or person performing similar functions) of (i) such member or sponsor, or (ii) a person which beneficially owns 50% or more of the equity interest in, or has the power to vote 50% or more of the voting interest in, such member or sponsor; (B) any person which beneficially owns or has the right to acquire 10% or more of the equity interest in or has the power to vote 10% or more of the voting interest in (i) such member or sponsor, or (ii) a person which beneficially owns 50% or more of the equity interest in, or has the power to vote 50% or more of the voting interest in, such member or sponsor; (C) any person with respect to which such member or sponsor, the persons specified in subparagraph (A) or (B), and the immediate families of partners, officers or directors (or persons performing similar functions) specified in subparagraph (A), or other person specified in subparagraph (B), in the aggregate beneficially own or have the right to acquire 10% or more of the equity interest or have the power to vote 10% or more of the voting interest; (D) any person an officer of which is also a person specified in subparagraph (A) or (B) and any person a majority of the board of directors of which is comprised of persons specified in subparagraph (A) or (B); or (E) any person controlled by a person or persons specified in subparagraphs (A), (B), (C) or (D). (2) Cash available for distribution — cash flow less amount set aside for restoration or creation of reserves. (3) Cash flow — cash funds provided from operations, including lease payments on net leases f
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a cap on fees, whereas the changed text removes any tracking or limits. The Law Explanation states that FINRA Rule 2310 mandates that compensation to underwriters and other sellers of securities be fair and reasonable. By removing the cap on fees, the changed text contradicts the intent to ensure fairness, potentially leading to unreasonable compensation. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing FINRA Rule 2310, supports the existence of this rule, and the modified text directly violates the principle of fair and reasonable compensation.
